hypothesis: Representations learned by diverse scientific foundation models—spanning architectures (equivariant vs non‑equivariant), input modalities (atomic/graph/SMILES/text), and domains (molecules, materials, proteins)—converge onto a shared low‑dimensional manifold that encodes the underlying physical and chemical structure of matter, producing high CKNNA and IDCor alignment for in‑distribution datasets and measurably reduced alignment for out‑of‑distribution samples.
